entities.name: ACHIEVE-1

attributes.primary_endpoint: change from baseline in glycated hemoglobin level at week 40
attributes.key_secondary_endpoint: percent change in body weight from baseline at week 40
attributes.patient_population: adults with type 2 diabetes managed with diet and exercise only, HbA1c 7.0-9.5%, BMI >= 23.0

entities.context: In this phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial, we randomly assigned participants in a 1:1:1:1 ratio to receive orforglipron at one of three doses (3 mg, 12 mg, or 36 mg) or placebo once daily for 40 weeks. (Supported by Eli Lilly; ACHIEVE-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05971940.)

entities.name: glycated hemoglobin
entities.context: The primary end point was the change from baseline to week 40 in the glycated hemoglobin level. The mean glycated hemoglobin level at baseline was 8.0%.

entities.name: body weight reduction
entities.context: The percent change in body weight from baseline to week 40 was -4.5% with the 3-mg dose, -5.8% with the 12-mg dose, -7.6% with the 36-mg dose, and -1.7% with placebo.

entities.name: Gastrointestinal Disorders
entities.context: The most common adverse events were mild-to-moderate gastrointestinal events, most of which occurred during dose escalation.

entities.name: Hypoglycemia
entities.context: No episodes of severe hypoglycemia were reported.

entities.name: Eli Lilly
entities.context: Supported by Eli Lilly; ACHIEVE-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05971940.

entities.name: Rosenstock et al., NEJM 2025

relations.evidence: Orforglipron is a small-molecule, nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist in clinical development for type 2 diabetes and weight management.
Orforglipron is a small-molecule, nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist in clinical development for type 2 diabetes and weight management.
Orforglipron is a small-molecule, nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist in clinical development for type 2 diabetes and weight management.
Orforglipron is a small-molecule, nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist in clinical development for type 2 diabetes and weight management.

In this phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial, we randomly assigned participants in a 1:1:1:1 ratio to receive orforglipron at one of three doses (3 mg, 12 mg, or 36 mg) or placebo once daily for 40 weeks.
The most common adverse events were mild-to-moderate gastrointestinal events, most of which occurred during dose escalation.
Supported by Eli Lilly; ACHIEVE-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05971940.
Supported by Eli Lilly; ACHIEVE-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05971940.

Title: Orforglipron, an Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist, in Early Type 2 Diabetes. Authors: Rosenstock Julio, Hsia Stanley, Nevarez Ruiz Luis, Eyde Sarah, Cox David. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2025). PMID: 40544435.
The primary end point was the change from baseline to week 40 in the glycated hemoglobin level. All three doses of orforglipron were superior to placebo with respect to the primary end point.
Participants had type 2 diabetes treated only with diet and exercise, a glycated hemoglobin level of at least 7.0% but no more than 9.5%.
